name: Stickynote Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Stickynote Workflow. This workflow integrates 6 different services: stickyNote, filter, code, scheduleTrigger, if. It contains 13 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3

parameters.content:
### 👨‍🎤 Setup
1. Add Todoist creds
2. Create a `template` list to copy from in Todoist. Add days and due times on each task as necessary.
3. Set the projects to copy from and to write to in each **Todoist** node

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
### 👆 This adds due dates to tasks from description.. 
### For example in the description of a task
`days:mon,tues; due:8am`
### So that it will create a task every Monday and Tuesday that's due at 8am ⏰

originalWorkflowJson.name: Stickynote Workflow
originalWorkflowJson.description: Automated workflow: Stickynote Workflow. This workflow integrates 6 different services: stickyNote, filter, code, scheduleTrigger, if. It contains 13 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
originalWorkflowJson.notes: Excellent quality workflow: Stickynote Workflow. This workflow has been optimized for production use with comprehensive error handling, security, and documentation.

nodes.name: Get all tasks from template project

parameters.content: ### 👈🏽 Every new task has `daily` label that gets deleted in the other flow

### 👆 This adds due dates to tasks from description.. 
### For example in the description of a task
`days:mon,tues; due:8am`
### So that it will create a task every Monday and Tuesday that's due at 8am ⏰

### 👨‍🎤 Setup
1. Add Todoist creds
2. Create a `template` list to copy from in Todoist. Add days and due times on each task as necessary.
3. Set the projects to copy from and to write to in each **Todoist** node

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
configurationSteps: Open the scheduler node "Every day at 5:10am".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the scheduler node "Every day at 5am".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get all tasks from template project" node (n8n-nodes-base.todoist) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Keep tasks that match today" node (n8n-nodes-base.filter) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get all tasks from Inbox" node (n8n-nodes-base.todoist) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "If list not empty" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "if it has daily label" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Delete task" node (n8n-nodes-base.todoist) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create new task in Inbox" node (n8n-nodes-base.todoist) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
